---
title: "Rhizosolenia / Proboscia"
subtitle: "Long, thin, cylindrical centric diatoms — solitary or in straight chains — with a drawn-out, tapering apical process."
categories: [Diatoms]
---

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build_pages.py — do not edit by hand. Edit meta.yml and re-run the script. -->

**Group:** Diatoms  ·  **Optics:** high-mag, low-mag

## Defining characteristics

Both genera are elongate, rod- to needle-like centric diatoms, many times longer than wide, occurring singly or in straight chains. Cells are cylindrical with numerous girdle bands and an obliquely conical apex drawn out into a fine process. In Rhizosolenia the apex bears an external process with small wing-like flaps (otaria) and paired claspers that link cells; in Proboscia the apex is extended into a curved, hollow "proboscis" and the claspers/otaria are absent. They are grouped here because at annotation resolution the two are routinely hard to separate. Key features: very high length-to-width ratio, cylindrical body, single tapering apical process.

## Distinguishing from similar classes

| Similar class | How to tell them apart |
|---|---|
| **chaetoceros** | Chaetoceros bears thin lateral setae from the valve corners and forms obvious chains with apertures; Rhizosolenia/Proboscia is a single long cylinder with one apical process, not lateral bristles. |
| **pennate** | Pennate diatoms are bilaterally symmetric; Rhizosolenia/Proboscia are centric (radially symmetric) cylinders. |

## Ideal images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## Challenging images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## References

- [Rhizosolenia — UCSC Ocean Data Center Phytoplankton Gallery](http://oceandatacenter.ucsc.edu/PhytoGallery/Diatoms/rhizosolenia.html)
- [Morphology of genus Proboscia and related Rhizosoleniaceae (Algae journal)](https://www.e-algae.org/journal/view.php?number=2644)
